input.background: We select an SRS of n=712 from a population of 20-29-year-old men and calculate x̄=183 pounds. The population standard deviation (σ) is 40.
input.question: We propose to calculate 90% confidence intervals for μ based on this information.

sample_size.description: The size of the simple random sample (SRS) taken from the population.

population_mean.description: The sample mean (x̄) calculated from the SRS.

population_std_dev.description: The population standard deviation (σ) provided for the calculation.

confidence_level.description: The confidence level for constructing the confidence interval.

meta_info.caption: Confidence intervals, various levels of confidence (Body weight, 20-29-year-old U.S. males)
meta_info.section: 10.2 Confidence Intervals for $\mu$ When $\sigma$ Is Known
